name: SharpHound

documentation.readme: The latest build of SharpHound will always be found here.

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: (Default: Default) Collection Methods: Container, Group, LocalGroup, GPOLocalGroup,

arguments.name: --cachename
arguments.description: Filename for cache (Defaults to a machine specific identifier)

arguments.name: --collectallproperties
arguments.description: Collect all LDAP properties from objects

arguments.name: --collectionmethods
arguments.description: (Default: Default) Collection Methods: Container, Group, LocalGroup, GPOLocalGroup,

arguments.name: --computerfile
arguments.description: Path to file containing computer names to enumerate

arguments.name: -d
arguments.description: Specify domain to enumerate

arguments.name: --disablecertverification
arguments.description: (Default: false) Disable certificate verification for secure LDAP

arguments.name: --disablesigning
arguments.description: (Default: false) Disables Kerberos Signing/Sealing

arguments.name: --distinguishedname
arguments.description: Base DistinguishedName to start the LDAP search at

arguments.name: --domain
arguments.description: Specify domain to enumerate

arguments.name: --domaincontroller
arguments.description: Override domain controller to pull LDAP from. This option can result in data loss

arguments.name: --excludedcs
arguments.description: (Default: false) Exclude domain controllers from session/localgroup enumeration (mostly for

arguments.name: -f
arguments.description: Add an LDAP filter to the pregenerated filter.

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: Display this help screen.

arguments.name: --jitter
arguments.description: Add jitter to throttle (percent)

arguments.name: -l
arguments.description: Loop computer collection

arguments.name: --ldappassword
arguments.description: Password for LDAP

arguments.name: --ldapport
arguments.description: (Default: 0) Override port for LDAP

arguments.name: --ldapusername
arguments.description: Username for LDAP

arguments.name: --localadminpassword
arguments.description: Specify the password of the localadmin for session enumeration

arguments.name: --localadminsessionenum
arguments.description: Specify if you want to use a dedicated LOCAL user for session enumeration

arguments.name: --localadminusername
arguments.description: Specify the username of the localadmin for session enumeration

arguments.name: --loopduration
arguments.description: Loop duration (hh:mm:ss - 05:00:00 is 5 hours, default: 2 hrs)

arguments.name: --loopinterval
arguments.description: Add delay between loops (hh:mm:ss - 00:03:00 is 3 minute)

arguments.name: --memcache
arguments.description: Keep cache in memory and don't write to disk

arguments.name: --nozip
arguments.description: (Default: false) Don't zip files

arguments.name: --outputdirectory
arguments.description: (Default: .) Directory to output file too

arguments.name: --outputprefix
arguments.description: String to prepend to output file names

arguments.name: --overrideusername
arguments.description: Override the username to filter for NetSessionEnum

arguments.name: --portchecktimeout
arguments.description: (Default: 500) Timeout for port checks in milliseconds

arguments.name: --prettyprint
arguments.description: (Default: false) Pretty print JSON

arguments.name: --randomfilenames
arguments.description: (Default: false) Use random filenames for output

arguments.name: --realdnsname
arguments.description: Override DNS suffix for API calls

arguments.name: --rebuildcache
arguments.description: (Default: false) Rebuild cache and remove all entries

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: (Default: false) Search all available domains in the forest

arguments.name: --searchforest
arguments.description: (Default: false) Search all available domains in the forest

arguments.name: --secureldap
arguments.description: (Default: false) Connect to LDAP SSL instead of regular LDAP

arguments.name: --skippasswordcheck
arguments.description: (Default: false) Skip PwdLastSet age check when checking computers

arguments.name: --skipportcheck
arguments.description: (Default: false) Skip checking if 445 is open

arguments.name: --skipregistryloggedon
arguments.description: Skip registry session enumeration

arguments.name: --statusinterval
arguments.description: (Default: 30000) Interval in which to display status in milliseconds

arguments.name: --stealth
arguments.description: Stealth Collection (Prefer DCOnly whenever possible!)

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: (Default: 50) Number of threads to run enumeration with

arguments.name: --throttle
arguments.description: Add a delay after computer requests in milliseconds

arguments.name: --trackcomputercalls
arguments.description: (Default: false) Adds a CSV tracking requests to computers

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: (Default: 2) Enable verbose output. Lower is more verbose

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: --zipfilename
arguments.description: Filename for the zip

arguments.name: --zippassword
arguments.description: Password protects the zip with the specified password

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

examples: dotnet build --tl:off               # To view CommonLib resolution logs
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dotnet build -p:CommonSource=Stable`. Further invocations from upstream: `dotnet build -p:CommonSource=Local`; `dotnet build --tl:off               # To view CommonLib resolution logs`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_windows, ad] in the Windows / AD category. Fits when the engagement needs: ![GitHub all releases](https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/SpecterOps/SharpHound/total).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: The latest build of SharpHound will always be found here.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/nishang (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/PowerSploit (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/Empire (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
